reasoning: The document contains 4 major defects: one from LLM fidelity regarding broken heading hierarchy, and three from programmatic evaluation including retained header/footer elements, truncated alt text on 16 images, and a table caption coded as a header row. Since the total number of major defects is exactly 4, this falls within the threshold for HUMAN_REVIEW.

concerns.analysis: The heading hierarchy is incorrectly assigned, causing major sections to be nested inside subsections. 'Coontail' is assigned as an H3 (correctly, as a species under 'Results and Discussion'). However, the major subsequent sections 'Conclusions and Management Implications' and 'References' are assigned as H4, incorrectly nesting them as subsections of 'Coontail'. This reverses the logical containment relationship of the document.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Conclusions and Management Implications (top-level section heading)

concerns.description: Table caption coded as header row: 1 table(s) have a single spanning first row that is likely a caption/title
concerns.analysis: caption_as_header=1. Examples: p6: "Roanoke Rapids". First row spans all columns — should be a <caption> element, not a header row.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (35): 2023 Roanoke Rapids Vegetation Survey Report; 1; 2023 Roanoke Rapids Vegetation Survey Report
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=35, pages_affected=18/19. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 16 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (16 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "...y with wispy clouds. A rocky, muddy shoreline with"; p1: "...in white uppercase letters. The words NC STATE are"; p7: "...nd brownish aquatic plants growing thickly beneath"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=16 (truncated=16, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

Checked for lost structural emphasis, specifically missing underlines, list markers, or demoted headings. On page 1, the text 'Prepared By:' and 'Submitted To:' are explicitly underlined in the source PDF. The JSON extracts these as italicized markdown (*Prepared By:*) but drops the underline emphasis. As stated in the guidelines, underline has no markdown equivalent, and dropping it constitutes a loss of emphasis.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 1 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=1, examples=['https://www.dominionenergy.com/projects-and-facilities/hydro']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=0, no_th_tables=1. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.

action_items: Correct heading levels for 'Conclusions and Management Implications' and 'References' to ensure proper document structure.
Remove retained header and footer text from the HTML output.
Rewrite the 16 truncated image alt texts to ensure full, meaningful descriptions.
Convert the spanning first row of the 'Roanoke Rapids' table into a proper <caption> element.
